_note: Reference claims hand-curated against the paper's masonry capture. Used as fallback when temporal_v1.py has not yet been run on a live frame sequence. Replaced automatically once temporal-results.json exists.

baseline._note: Baseline VLM gets one frame at a time and is asked 'what changed since the previous frame?' — but the model has no access to the previous frame, so it can only describe THIS frame. The output reads as state-change claims but is structurally incapable of grounding them. This is the failure mode vima's multi-frame architecture solves.

per_frame_claims.claim: worker is laying a concrete block; cannot describe a change without a prior reference
wall has visible courses; previous frame not available, change unconfirmed
worker placing a block; appears to be ongoing work
worker visible on lift platform; cannot compare to previous
worker on scaffold; no visual reference to compare against
wall continues to be built; no prior frame for change detection
scaffold and guardrail visible; cannot tell what changed
worker working at elevation; comparison frame unavailable

claims.description: wall section grew by three courses between t=0s and t=183s
claims.evidence: frame 0 shows worker placing first course block; frame 1 shows three full courses laid in the same wall segment

claims.description: primary block pallet diminished from full to ~40% remaining
claims.evidence: block stack height drops from ~12 courses visible to ~5; pattern matches active masonry consumption

claims.description: primary worker moved from ground-level to scaffold platform
claims.evidence: frame 2 shows worker at ground placing block; frame 4 shows worker on scaffold plank, hands on a higher course

claims.description: open edge appears on scaffold north side after material relocation
claims.evidence: frame 4 shows guardrail in place; frame 6 shows guardrail removed for material delivery, no harness on worker

claims.description: frames 6 and 7 show identical worker pose and material state
claims.evidence: drift only — no grounded change to claim

refusals.reason: lighting changed substantially (cloud cover) but no scene-level change is grounded in two frames
